seo.title: Building Data Extraction with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: DSPy enables programming LLMs for data extraction by composing optimizable modules that generate structured outputs from unstructured documents. Guardrails are essential to catch common failures like schema violations, inaccurate field extraction, and incomplete outputs that optimized prompts often miss. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure reliable production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
DSPy data extraction typically uses Signature modules to define input/output schemas, ChainOfThought for reasoning, and Program optimizers to tune prompts against training data. A basic pipeline chains document parsing, entity extraction, and validation modules. Outputs are Pydantic models or JSON matching predefined schemas for downstream consumption.

The flow starts with a raw document fed into a dspy.Signature defining fields like 'name', 'amount', 'date'. Modules are compiled with optimizers like BootstrapFewShot, then executed via program.forward(). Production risks emerge when optimized chains produce schema-invalid or hallucinated extractions.

Vex wraps module calls to verify schema compliance, field accuracy against ground truth samples, and completeness at inference time.

risks.name: Optimized prompts overfitting to training examples
risks.description: DSPy optimizers create prompts too specific to training data, failing on real documents with varied formats
risks.mitigation: Vex schema verification rejects format-specific hallucinations regardless of training data

risks.name: Chain-of-thought reasoning producing plausible but wrong logic
risks.description: CoT modules generate convincing reasoning chains that extract incorrect values from ambiguous text
risks.mitigation: Vex field accuracy checks validate extracted values against document content and external sources

risks.name: Assertion violations not caught at inference time
risks.description: DSPy lacks runtime schema enforcement, allowing malformed JSON or missing required fields
risks.mitigation: Vex wraps forward() calls with comprehensive schema compliance assertions

risks.name: Module composition creating unexpected output distributions
risks.description: Chained modules amplify errors, producing inconsistent field types or incomplete extractions
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness validation ensures all required fields present with correct types

implementation.title: Define Extraction Signature
implementation.description: Create DSPy Signature for invoice data extraction with typed fields
implementation.code:
import dspy

class InvoiceExtractor(dspy.Signature):
    """Extracts invoice details from text."""
    
    document: str = dspy.InputField()
    name: str = dspy.OutputField(desc="Invoice recipient name")
    amount: float = dspy.OutputField(desc="Total invoice amount")
    date: str = dspy.OutputField(desc="Invoice date in YYYY-MM-DD")
    invoice_id: str = dspy.OutputField(desc="Unique invoice ID")

extractor = InvoiceExtractor()

# Usage
result = extractor(document="Invoice #INV-123 to Acme Corp... $1,250.00 on 2024-03-15")
print(result)

implementation.title: Build Extraction Program
implementation.description: Compose ChainOfThought program and optimize with few-shot examples
implementation.code:
import dspy

co_t_extractor = dspy.ChainOfThought(InvoiceExtractor)

# Training examples
trainset = [
    dspy.Example(document="Invoice #ABC123 to XYZ Ltd $500.00 2024-01-15", 
                 name="XYZ Ltd", amount=500.0, date="2024-01-15", invoice_id="ABC123").with_inputs("document")
]

# Optimize
optimizer = dspy.BootstrapFewShot(metric=dspy.evaluate.answer_exact_match)
optimized_extractor = optimizer.compile(co_t_extractor, trainset=trainset)

# Execute
result = optimized_extractor(document="Invoice INV-456 Acme Inc $2,300 2024-04-01")
print(result)

implementation.title: Structured Output Validation
implementation.description: Add Pydantic model for runtime type checking post-extraction
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional
from datetime import date

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    name: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    amount: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    date: str = Field(regex=r'^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$')
    invoice_id: str = Field(..., pattern=r'^INV-\d+$')

# Parse DSPy output
invoice_data = {
    'name': result.name,
    'amount': float(result.amount),
    'date': result.date,
    'invoice_id': result.invoice_id
}

try:
    validated_invoice = Invoice(**invoice_data)
    print("✓ Valid invoice:", validated_invoice)
except Exception as e:
    print("✗ Validation failed:", e)

implementation.title: Batch Processing Pipeline
implementation.description: Process multiple documents with error handling and logging

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Module Calls with Vex Verify
vexIntegration.description: Basic integration wrapping DSPy forward() with schema verification
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.verify(
    assertions=[
        vex.schema_matches('Invoice'),
        vex.field_accuracy('amount', tolerance=0.01),
        "len(name) > 0",
        "invoice_id.startswith('INV-')"
    ],
    risk_profile='medium'
)
def verified_extractor(document: str) -> dict:
    result = optimized_extractor(document)
    return {
        'name': result.name,
        'amount': float(result.amount),
        'date': result.date,
        'invoice_id': result.invoice_id
    }

# Usage - fails fast on violations
result = verified_extractor("Invoice #INV-123 to Acme Corp $1250 2024-03-15")
print(result)

vexIntegration.title: Completeness + Type Verification
vexIntegration.description: Ensure all required fields present with correct types and formats

vexIntegration.title: Production Pipeline with Vex Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Full pipeline with failure mode detection and alerting
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

@vex.verify(
    assertions=[
        vex.schema_matches('Invoice'),
        vex.field_accuracy('amount'),
        vex.completeness(['name', 'amount', 'date', 'invoice_id']),
        "len(name.strip()) >= 2"
    ],
    risk_profile='medium',
    alert_on_fail=True,
    metric_tags={'pipeline': 'invoice_extraction'}
)
def production_pipeline(document: str, doc_id: str) -> Invoice:
    """Production-ready extraction with full verification."""
    raw_result = optimized_extractor(document)
    
    invoice = Invoice(
        name=raw_result.name,
        amount=float(raw_result.amount),
        date=raw_result.date,
        invoice_id=raw_result.invoice_id
    )
    
    logger.info(f"Extracted invoice {doc_id}: {invoice.invoice_id}")
    return invoice

# Batch processing
invoices = []
for doc_id, doc in documents.items():
    try:
        invoice = production_pipeline(doc, doc_id)
        invoices.append(invoice)
    except vex.VerificationError as e:
        logger.error(f"Vex blocked invalid extraction for {doc_id}: {e}")
